Chapter XXII—Retention of Embryo
^tqynxvri fomr: i
Wru: u?M
The four days commencing from the 8th lunar day of the light
half of Jyestha month are sustained by the winds, and will prove
beneficial, if they be attended with soft and auspicious breeze, and
if the sky be covered with glossy clouds.
[The idea is that the four days mentioned here are helpful in
retaining the embryo by means of the winds. Hence the days are
called Vtyiudhdranas. The word is construed by the commentator
as 'the wind blowing from the north, north-east and east'.]
